explanation:
**Personal finance and investing** use universal mathematical principles (compound interest, time value of money, risk-return optimization). This calculator applies standard industry formulas.

## How it calculates

Calculate CEDEAR dividend yield with USD dividends.

This calculator applies the formula with your inputs, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unusual values.

## Typical benchmarks by timeframe

| Timeframe | S&P 500 | 10y Bonds | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4-5% | 3-5% |
| 5 years | ~8% avg | ~4% | Inflation |
| 10 years | ~9-10% | ~4% | <inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Investing principles

- **Stick to your plan** during market downturns. Emotion is your worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: don't concentrate in one asset, sector, or country.
- **Keep costs low**: expense ratios >1% annually eat 30% of your returns over 30 years.
- **Think long-term**: stocks work best with 10+ year horizons, not 1 year.
- **Rebalance annually**: once per year, no more.

## Final notes

This calculation is a reference tool only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values updated through 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.a: Argentine Depositary Certificate: represents a fractional share of foreign stocks traded in Argentina.
How many CEDEARs equal one real share. Example: AAPL ratio 10 means 10 CEDEARs = 1 Apple share.
Dividends are paid in USD and typically converted to ARS at MEP rates through your broker.

faq.q: What taxes apply to CEDEAR dividends?
faq.a: Argentina levies a 15% tax on dividend income for individuals.

faq.q: Is there US withholding tax on CEDEAR dividends?
faq.a: Potentially 30% withholding at source unless you file Form W-8BEN with your broker.
Reinvestment helps compound growth over time. Many brokers offer automatic dividend reinvestment.

faq.q: What's the difference between dividend yield and stock price appreciation?
faq.a: Dividend yield measures income from dividends only, not gains from stock price increases.

faq.q: What returns should I expect from the stock market?
faq.a: S&P 500 historical average: ~10% nominal annually (7% real after inflation). Past performance doesn't predict future results but serves as a reference.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology
